audit_items.proof_trace: In `ldap_create_dirsync_control` (L99) and `ldap_create_extended_dn_control` (L246), the guard checks only `ctrlp == NULL` but immediately dereferences `ld->ld_errno` without first checking `ld == NULL`; if both parameters are NULL the function crashes. This is inconsistent with sibling functions `ldap_create_dirsync_value` and `ldap_parse_dirsync_control` which explicitly handle `ld == NULL` before any dereference, establishing that the library design allows NULL `ld` as an error condition.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An application using libldap that calls `ldap_create_dirsync_control(NULL, ..., NULL)` or `ldap_create_extended_dn_control(NULL, ..., NULL)` — e.g., after a failed `ldap_initialize` that returns NULL — will trigger a null pointer dereference crash (SIGSEGV) at the `ld->ld_errno` assignment.
